rulings.Case Title: HENRIETTA MARTIN VS. DAVID DEMO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants David Demo And Susan Demo'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Production Of Documents And Request For Fees, Costs And Monetary Sanctions Against Henrietta Martin And Her Attorney Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2014, Line 1 - Defendants David Demo And Susan Demos' Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Production Of Documents And Request For Fees, Costs And Monetary Sanctions Against Henrietta Martin And Her Attorney Of Record. Defendants' Motion to Compel Further Discovery Responses is DENIED, in part and GRANTED, in part. (1) Motion to Compel Further responses to request for Production of Documents Nos. 1 - 44 is denied. It appears from the papers that Plaintiff has produced all responsive documents and is withholding none based up the objections stated in her response. (2) Plaintiff's objections to Request for Production Nos 45, 46 & 46(a) are overruled and full and complete responses will be provided to defendant within twenty (20) days from the date of this order. (3) Plaintiff is ordered to serve within twenty (20) days from the date of this order a verification of all her supplemental discovery responses and based on an assumption that Plaintiff will comply, Defendant' motion is denied as to Nos. 6, 8, 28, 31, 43, 55, 64, 66, 67, 68 & 69. Plaintiff is ordered to serve within twenty (20) days from the date of this order a response to Special Interrogatory No. 7. Plaintiff has supplemented her responses to Defendant's Requests for Admission Nos 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 31, 32, 33 & 344. To the extent that any of the responses are other than unqualified admissions, Plaintiff within twenty (20) days from the date of this order will provide to Defendant the information called for by Form Interrogatory 17.1. (4) Plaintiff is ordered to serve within twenty (20) days from the date of this order full and complete responses to Form Interrogatories Nos. 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.6, 9.1, 17.1 & 50.2. (5) Monetary sanctions are assessed against Plaintiff in the amount of $2,500.00 with payment to be made to defendant and their Counsel within ten (10) days from the date of this order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LAURENCE FOK et al VS. PETER J TOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2014, Line 11- Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Discovery is Granted. Within 10 days of the hearing date, Defendants shall serve verified supplemental responses and produce responsive documents to Request for Production nos. 7 - 11, 14, 15, 24 and 25. Monetary sanctions are denied. The matter has been delayed due to Defendants' former counsel and is not the result of an abuse of discovery by Defendants. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JJJ, LLC VS. CASIP, INC., FORMERLY CASIP, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene And Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2014, Line 5- INTERVENOR TARIQ ALAZRAIE'S Motion To Intervene And Vacate Judgment. Intervenor's Motion to Intervene and Vacate Judgment is DENIED. Moving arty fails to sustain burden under CCP 387 and CCP 473. The entry of judgment was not caused by non-party's failure to file a complaint in intervention. Motion to intvenes is not timely and Plaintiff demonstrated prejudice "other than being required to prove their case." Truck Ins. Exchange v. Superior Court (Transco Syndicate No. 1) (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 342, 351. Additionally, Defendant failed to employ the Claims of Right to Possession procedure designed specifically for situations where unnanmed person claim an interest in the leased property. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SALLY LIU VS. CHADWICK HOWARD et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2014, Line 7 -DEFENDANTS CHADWICK HOWARD and PATRICIA NESBITS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with 5-day leave to amend to allege when the operative notices were served and when they expired. First Amended Complaint is uncertain as it alleges several different dates of the purported service, e.g. December 8, 2014; December 29, 2013 and January 6-8, 2014. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRO CULTURAL SERVICO DE LAS AMERICAS VS. ROBERT LO VERSO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2014, Line 9 -DEFENDANT ROBERT VERSO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for Unlawful Detainer. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED for what it is worth. Motion to Quash is DENIED, it is untimely per CCP 1167.4 and it is not supported by any grounds in the purported Notice of Motion (Document captioned "Defendant's Demurrer to Plaintiff's Unlawful Detainer Complaint¿") and by any authorities in the moving papers. Request for sanctions under CC 1942.5 is improper at this time. Defendants and their counsel are ordered to comply with CRC in future (separate Notice and Motion and bind the documents). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JABARI MAGNUS VS. CHARLES BANDIERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To: 1) Compel Defendant'S Responses, Without Objection, To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories- General, Set One, Form Interrogatories- Unlawful Detainer, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, 2) Deem Admissions, And 3) Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 20, 2014, Line 10- PLAINTIFF JABARI MAGNUS' Motion To: 1) Compel Defendant'S Responses, Without Objection, To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories- General, Set One, Form Interrogatories- Unlawful Detainer, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, 2) Deem Admissions, And 3) Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Discovery is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Deem Admissions admitted and for evidentiary sanctions is denied. Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery is GRANTED. Defendant shall provide complete responses without objections to Requests for Production, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Requests for Admissions by hand delivery to Plaintiff's counsel's office within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of $1,585. = (501/REQ)
